phonetic: k蓽藧藞k蕣k; 藞k蓽藧k蕣k
meaning: 鍩哄皵搴撳厠[浼婃媺鍏嬩笢鍖楅儴鍩庡競]
examples: Another person was killed in Kirkuk in what appeared to be the deadliest day in Iraq in more than a year.
Elections in the disputed city of Kirkuk were to be postponed until a separate agreement could be reached.
The first index case of cholera, confirmed by laboratory test, was reported from Kirkuk Governate on 14 August 2007.
Also in the north, U.S. special forces discovered a large weapons cache just south of Kirkuk.
Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice made a surprise visit to Kirkuk in northern Iraq today.
